prompt: This program throws NullPointerException on the println line. What is the bug?
choices: Node fields must be initialized in a constructor before use
b is declared but never assigned — it is uninitialized, and accessing b.value is a compile error (local variable b may not have been initialized)
String fields default to null and cannot be used in println
a.value should be used instead of b.value
explanation: Local variables in Java are not given default values — they must be initialized before use. The compiler catches this as 'variable b might not have been initialized' and refuses to compile. Fix: Node b = new Node(); or Node b = null; (though then b.value would still throw NullPointerException at runtime).
